title: Basic
summary: Defines multilinear ambient inputs/operators and basic operations for replacing or transforming input slots.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines multilinear ambient inputs/operators and basic operations for replacing or transforming input slots.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in PolyGJMSoperators/Basic.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Multi Input. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev MultiInput (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) (r : ℕ) : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Multi Operator. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev MultiOperator (CalConf : Calculus Conf) (r : ℕ) : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Unary Multi Operator. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: Bi Operator. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: Tri Operator. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: unary To Multi. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def unaryToMulti (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) : CalConf.MultiOperator 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: multi To Unary. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def multiToUnary (P : CalConf.MultiOperator 1) : Function Conf → Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: unary To Multi apply. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem unaryToMulti_apply (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput 1) : CalConf.unaryToMulti P F = P (F 0)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: multi To Unary apply. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem multiToUnary_apply (P : CalConf.MultiOperator 1) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.multiToUnary P f = P (fun _ => f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: replace. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def replace {r : ℕ} (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.MultiInput r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: map Unary. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def mapUnary {r : ℕ} (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : CalConf.MultiInput r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: replace same. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem replace_same {r : ℕ} (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) (f : Function Conf) : replace F i f i = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: map Unary apply. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mapUnary_apply {r : ℕ} (P : Function Conf → Function Conf) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (i : Fin r) : mapUnary P F i = P (F i)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
